ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a filled flaky pastry foldover with chicken, mushroom, and cheese fondue. FOODON_03315091 (chicken meat food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (Pastry products) is the closest defensible parent category. No specific code exists for a chicken-filled foldover pastry.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a commercial chicken foldover pastry product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Creamy Chicken Foldover pastry, approximately 130 g, as sold at Starbucks Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Pie / Savoury Chicken Pastry — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Chicken pot pie, frozen, prepared (NDB 21065) and Puff pastry shell, baked (NDB 18345), combined estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for a Starbucks Singapore Creamy Chicken Foldover in HPB Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for a baked savoury chicken pastry (chicken pot pie, prepared, NDB 21065) as the closest defensible analogue. Macros adjusted to reflect a richer cream-and-cheese filling consistent with the product description. Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the assembled product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pastry and savoury pie products — analogous estimate based on shortcrust/puff pastry GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Starbucks Creamy Chicken Foldover. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for baked savoury pastry products (puff/shortcrust pastry, GI range 50–59). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 33.15) / 100 ≈ 18.2, which is a medium glycemic load.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~546 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this pastry, as one serving provides approximately 546 mg sodium (~24% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and the processed cheese fondue filling further contributes to sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 55) but moderate glycemic load (~18 per serving) and refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this pastry in moderation; despite its low GI of approximately 55, the moderate glycemic load of ~18 per 130 g serving and refined pastry dough can still contribute to a postprandial glucose rise, especially given the limited dietary fibre.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~7.5 g per serving) and sodium (~546 mg per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit intake of this pastry, as one serving contributes approximately 7.5 g of saturated fat (~38% of a 20 g daily limit) and significant sodium, both of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and elevated blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Chicken Foldover is a baked savoury pastry sold at Starbucks Singapore outlets. It consists of a flaky, laminated pastry shell that is folded over a filling of tender chicken chunks, sautéed mushrooms, and a rich cheese fondue cream sauce. The pastry exterior is golden and crisp, while the interior is moist, creamy, and savoury. It is served warm and is positioned as a light meal or snack item in the Starbucks Singapore food menu. Nutritionally, one 130 g serving provides approximately 345 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 14.5 g fat (of which ~5.8 g is saturated), and 25.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The sodium content is notable at approximately 420 mg per 100 g, making it a moderate-to-high sodium food. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), with a glycemic load of approximately 18 per serving, relevant for blood glucose management. The product contains gluten (wheat pastry), dairy (cheese fondue, cream), and likely egg in the pastry, making it unsuitable for those with these allergens. It is not certified halal, vegetarian, or vegan. Clinically, it is a moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI but moderate GL and refined carbohydrate content, and a high concern for those with hypertension due to its sodium load. It is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet.
common_user_queries: What is in the Starbucks Creamy Chicken Foldover?
How many calories does a Starbucks chicken foldover have?
Is the Starbucks chicken foldover healthy?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for the Starbucks Singapore Creamy Chicken Foldover was found in HPB Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (chicken pot pie, prepared) with adjustments for the richer cheese fondue filling. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for baked savoury pastry products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy analogue source used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the analogue source. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamins A, B1, B2, B3, B6, B12, folate, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy analogue and should be treated as approximate. The halal status of the Starbucks Singapore Creamy Chicken Foldover is not confirmed from an approved source; is_halal is set to false pending verification. Serving size of 130 g is an estimate based on typical Starbucks Singapore savoury pastry portion sizes; the exact weight should be verified against the product label. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (55 × 25.5 × 1.30) / 100 ≈ 18.2, which is a medium glycemic load.
